channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Cape Town dam levels rise significantly after recent heavy rains
item.description: Following intense storms between 10 and 11 May, Cape Town has experienced a welcome boost in dam levels, which have increased by 12.4%, bringing the total capacity to 61.2%. Officials expect levels to...

item.title: Daily Life in Kyoto in the Early 1900s Through Vintage Photographs
item.description: This article presents a collection of vintage photographs showcasing everyday life in Kyoto, Japan, during the early 20th century. The images capture the delicate balance between tradition and the beg...

item.title: Reimagining South Africa: Towards a Second Republic
item.description: Three decades after democracy, South Africa continues to struggle with unresolved issues of land dispossession, inequality, and economic exclusion. Muzi Sikhakhane argues that the country is at a crit...

item.title: CNN commentator criticizes inclusion of Brett Ratner in Trump's official China delegation
item.description: During President Donald Trump's official trip to China, filmmaker Brett Ratner accompanied the U.S. delegation, traveling on Air Force One at taxpayer expense. CNN commentator Ana Navarro strongly cri...

item.title: PITX to waive terminal fees for all public utility vehicles for three months
item.description: Starting May 18, 2026, all public utility vehicles (PUVs) using the Parañaque Integrated Terminal Exchange (PITX) will no longer be charged terminal fees for a period of three months. The Department o...

item.title: Cape Town property owners face potential overpayments due to misclassified municipal rates
item.description: A significant financial discrepancy has been identified in numerous municipal accounts across the City of Cape Town, which could affect property owners who are unaware of potential overpayments. Steer...

item.title: APC shifts House of Reps primaries amid uncertainty over cleared aspirants
item.description: The ruling All Progressives Congress (APC) don postpone their House of Representatives primaries wey suppose hold on Friday to Saturday because plenty confusion dey around the clearance of aspirants a...

item.title: Wike denies PDP-APM alliance and dismisses Makinde’s presidential bid
item.description: Nyesom Wike, Minister of the Federal Capital Territory and national leader of a PDP faction, don dismiss talk wey dey claim say PDP don form alliance with Allied Peoples Movement (APM). Wike yarn say ...

item.title: Noida Labour Protest Accused Allegedly Received Funds from Foreign Countries
item.description: The recent Noida labour protest has come under the scrutiny of law enforcement authorities following reports that the accused individuals involved in the protest allegedly received over Rs 1 crore fro...

item.title: Palestinian family's land in West Bank advertised as holiday rental in Israeli settlement
item.description: Mohammad al-Sbeih’s family once farmed a small hillside plot south of Bethlehem, cultivating wheat and barley for three generations. That land is now part of the Israeli settlement Neve Daniel, and Bo...

item.title: Tanzania Secures 4th Place in Africa for Mining Investment Despite Policy Challenges
item.description: Tanzania imeonyesha maendeleo makubwa katika kuvutia uwekezaji wa madini, kufikia nafasi ya 4 barani Afrika kwa mwaka wa 2025 kulingana na uchunguzi wa Fraser Institute. Nchi hii ipo 34 kati ya nchi 6...

item.title: Daily Feng Shui Guidance by Master Hanz Cua for May 15, 2026
item.description: On May 15, 2026, Master Hanz Cua, a recognized feng shui expert in the Philippines, provides his daily insights and guidance based on feng shui principles. His predictions focus on helping individuals...

item.title: Power Grids in Luzon and Visayas Placed on Red Alert Due to Strained Supply
item.description: The National Grid Corp. of the Philippines (NGCP) has raised the alert status of power grids in Luzon and the Visayas, signaling ongoing stress in electricity supply. The red alert, effective from 3 p...

item.title: Kenya Wins First Medals at African Championships in Accra
item.description: Kenya kicked off its medal campaign at the African Championships held in Accra, Ghana, with impressive performances from Kelvin Chesang and Silas Senchura. Chesang clinched gold in the men's 10,000m r...

item.title: Arusha Residents Get Free Health Services as Nurses Celebrate World Nurses Day
item.description: On Thursday, May 14, 2026, over 2,000 residents of Arusha Region benefitted from free medical services at the Arusha Lutheran Medical Centre (ALMC) as part of World Nurses Day celebrations. The event ...

item.title: Analyst says Senate proceedings could have been handled more smoothly
item.description: An analyst has remarked that the recent events in the Philippine Senate, described as a 'spectacle' by some observers, could have been prevented with better planning and adherence to procedural rules....

item.title: Apple rolls out iOS 26.5 with encrypted RCS messaging and security fixes
item.description: Apple has released iOS 26.5 for supported iPhones, bringing one of the most anticipated messaging upgrades to the platform. The standout addition is end-to-end encrypted RCS messaging, currently rolli...

item.title: US Authorities Expected to Drop Fraud Charges Against Gautam Adani Following Investment Proposal
item.description: The US Justice Department and Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) are reportedly planning to drop the fraud and bribery charges against Indian billionaire Gautam Adani, which were initially filed...

item.title: Modi Holds Private Talks with Russian FM Lavrov, Iranian FM Araghchi Meets NSA Doval
item.description: In New Delhi, Russian Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov had a private meeting with Prime Minister Narendra Modi, while Iran's Foreign Minister Seyed Abbas Araghchi met separately with National Security A...

item.title: Split Enz celebrates 50th anniversary with reunion concert
item.description: The article could not be accessed due to News Corp Australia's crawler bot restrictions. It was meant to cover Split Enz’s 50th anniversary reunion concert, highlighting the band’s performance and fan...
